MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Deborah A. Smith, Esq. of GORDON & REES (415-875-3383), appearing for defendant / moving party Siemens Energy & Automation, Inc.
James P. Nevin, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Mark Johnson, et al.

RULING:
Defendant Siemens Energy & Automation, Inc.'s motion for protective order. Matter not argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion is granted in part and denied in part. The deposition may be taken at a location within 30 miles of Siemens' designated COR/PMK and all parties may appear by telephone. Siemens must produce all non-privileged responsive documents at least five days before the deposition and its PMK may be deposed as to all categories listed in the notice. As to 1960 through 1969, there should be no burden to Siemens since it claims not to have been in existence at that time. As to any later period, Siemens has not demonstrated that compliance will involve any undue burden. Order signed in open court per parties' agreement. Judge: Harold Kahn, Matter not reported

Off Calendar Feb-18-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Anne T. Acuna, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Mark Johnson and Lynne Johnson
Deborah A. Smith, Esq. of GORDON & REES (415-986-5900), appearing for defendant / opposing party Siemens Energy & Automation, Inc.

RULING:
Plaintiffs MARK JOHNSON & LYNNE JOHNSON's motion to compel the deposition of defendant Siemens Energy & Automation, Inc.'s person most knowledgeable and custodian of records and to compel the production of documents. Argued and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Plaintiffs' motion is granted. Plaintiffs are entitled to such discovery as it is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence and not otherwise subject to objection. If Siemens cannot located the documents requested after a diligent search, its custodian of records should so state. Deposition of custodian is stayed pending the ruling of defendant's motion for protective order and the ruling on the scope and location. Parties are to meet and confer about the issues and to submit an order for the Court's consideration. Judge: Harold Kahn, Matter not reported
